items.title: US and Iran launch new strikes, as Kuwait says airport hit by Iranian drones
items.summary: Kuwait says one person is dead after drones hit civilian buildings including its international airport.

items.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon but partial truce with Hezbollah appears to hold
items.summary: Israel continues its campaign in the south, though has not struck Beirut after a deal announced by the US.

items.title: Eight killed after drone hits bus in Russia-controlled part of Ukraine
items.summary: The bus was travelling between Moscow and Simferopol, in Russian-controlled Crimea, an official says.

items.title: Putin remains uncompromising on Ukraine, but is public discourse on war changing in Russia?
items.summary: Russia is intensifying attacks in Ukraine but more than four years of war are causing concern even among Putin loyalists.

items.title: Shell pumped oil through Nigeria pipeline for years despite pollution evidence, documents show
items.summary: The oil giant says the documents ignore the critical context of the complex operating environment at the time.

items.title: Trump administration drops $1.8bn 'anti-weaponisation' fund
items.summary: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche's announcement to abandon the fund comes amid pushback from top Republican lawmakers.

items.title: Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire
items.summary: Many victims were South Asians who had travelled to India for treatment or to accompany relatives.

items.title: Mayor cancels DR Congo friendly with Chile in Spain over Ebola concern
items.summary: DR Congo's international friendly against Chile in Spain next week is cancelled after authorities raise health concerns over the Ebola outbreak in the African nation.

items.title: Watch: Man attacked by bear at steel works in Japan
items.summary: The bear, which injured four people in the attack on Tuesday, remains on the loose within the factory compound.

items.title: British couple lose Iran jail sentence appeal, family says
items.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman were arrested in January 2025 during a global motorbike journey.

items.title: Microsoft says new quantum chip 1,000 times more reliable than predecessor
items.summary: The tech giant predicts it will have a quantum computer that can solve commercially useful problems by the end of the decade.

items.title: Mysteriously missing congressman wins primary after Trump endorsement
items.summary: Republican lawmaker Tom Kean Jr has not been seen in Washington or his New Jersey district for several months.

items.title: Maga influencer admits London Tube station assault
items.summary: Melissa Rein Lively accepted a caution, while an assault by beating charge against her was withdrawn.

items.title: Canada formally requests 16-year renewal of North American free trade pact
items.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

items.title: Two arrested after four migrant farm workers killed in Italy minivan fire
items.summary: CCTV images showed two people blocking the van's doors from the outside and throwing liquid inside to start a fire.

items.title: South Africa police investigate killing of two Mozambican men
items.summary: The deaths come after a day of violent protests against illegal migration in the town of Mossel Bay.

items.title: Trump names housing official Bill Pulte as acting US spymaster
items.summary: The head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency, who pushed for criminal charges against Trump's foes, will step in as acting Director of National Intelligence.

items.title: Sailors stressed and exhausted after months trapped by Strait of Hormuz blockade
items.summary: The uncertainty has weighed heavily on the 20,000 seafarers trapped in the Iran war zone.

items.title: 'They'll fix the building, but not our souls': Sleepy Kyiv neighbourhood hit in Russian strike
items.summary: Scenes of devastation greet residents emerging from underground shelters after a major Russian attack.

items.title: He was a reality show villain. Can he be the mayor of one of America's largest cities?
items.summary: Republican Spencer Pratt, known for his TV role on The Hills, appears to be in a tight race with two Democrats in Los Angeles.

items.title: 'Mornings and nights no longer exist' at 47C:  A day in the hottest place in India
items.summary: What a day in India's hottest district reveals about life on the frontline of extreme heat.

items.title: Watch: Explosion at fireworks factory in Malta
items.summary: Two men were injured in the explosion and were taken to hospital with minor injuries.

items.title: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
items.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

items.title: Marilyn Monroe 'lookalikes' gather to celebrate her 100th birthday
items.summary: Fans set a new world record for the largest gathering of people dressed as Marilyn Monroe to celebrate what would have been the legendary actress’ centenary.

items.title: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
items.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.
